name: Cybereason
description: Cybereason is an endpoint detection and response (EDR) platform that provides real-time threat detection, MalOp (Malicious Operation) investigation, and automated incident response across enterprise endpoints.

config.setupInstructions:
Prerequisites:
- Cybereason management console access with admin privileges
- The user MUST be created as type 'API' (not 'GUI/Console' user). GUI users with 2FA enabled cannot be used for this integration.

Steps:
1. Log in to your Cybereason management console as an admin
2. Navigate to the admin area (typically System > Users or Settings > Users depending on your version)
3. Create a new user of type 'API' (not GUI/Console user)
4. Assign the API user the 'System Admin' role for full API access
5. Enter your Cybereason server URL below (e.g., https://yourcompany.cybereason.net)
6. Enter the API user credentials

Note: The integration authenticates via session cookies. API-type users are designed for programmatic access and do not require 2FA, unlike GUI users which require interactive two-factor authentication.

credentialFields.label: Server URL
credentialFields.helpText: Your Cybereason server URL (e.g., https://yourcompany.cybereason.net). This is the base URL of your Cybereason management console.

credentialFields.label: API Username
credentialFields.helpText: The username for your Cybereason API user. Must be an API-type user (not a GUI/Console user).

credentialFields.label: Password
credentialFields.helpText: The password for your Cybereason API user.

checks.name: Secure Devices
checks.description: Verifies that endpoint protection sensors are actively running on monitored devices. Checks the status of all sensors and flags any that are disconnected, stale, or have outdated protection versions.

checks.name: Device List
checks.description: Lists all monitored endpoints and sensors in the Cybereason environment. Provides a comprehensive inventory of all devices under endpoint protection management.

checks.name: Monitoring and Alerting
checks.description: Checks that Cybereason is actively detecting and generating MalOps (Malicious Operations) and security alerts. Verifies that the detection engine is operational and threats are being identified.

checks.name: Incident Response
checks.description: Verifies that security incidents (MalOps) are being tracked, investigated, and remediated. Checks for unresolved incidents and validates that response workflows are in place.
